topic: NMP deceleration in Siberian sector — c4 verify-patch run
change_summary: Decider self-applied c3 Hole-1 fix in commit 539f4228 (2026-05-08T05:21Z): replaced 'no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all' absolute with the c3-recommended careful phrasing about Domain 3 + Domain NMP Longitude monitoring scalars rather than sector-bound deceleration. Hole-2 (Livermore citation precision) and Hole-3 (Section 6.5 cross-ref) explicitly carried forward as remaining work (decider commit message: 'Remaining: ISS-1878 (Livermore citation precision, moderate)'). c4 verifies the patch and re-examines residual holes with fresh eyes.
our_argument_summary: WIN-059 is Std Model Explains: dome's NMP drift rate prediction (Kill-Shot Test 6) shows 39.9% error and only PASSes via tolerance widening from a tighter prior value to 50%. The dome's H(r)=8,537·exp(−r/8,619) field is axially symmetric and cannot derive sector-specific behavior. Recent NMP deceleration is explained by Livermore-2020-class flux-lobe dynamics on the core-mantle boundary. The same NOAA NP.xy dataset is reused across WIN-006/007/036/043/059/063 to inflate confirmation count. Post-c3 patch: detail_verdict_text now articulates that Domain 3 monitors only scalar drift rate (currently 39.9% off) and Domain NMP Longitude monitors only a single position scalar — neither validates the sector-specific deceleration interpretation as a dome-derived prediction.

patch_verification.c3_hole_1_evidence: Old phrase 'no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all' — confirmed REMOVED from current data/wins.json:detail_verdict_text. New phrase 'no monitoring entry validates the WIN-specific claim' — confirmed PRESENT and matches c3 recommendation verbatim down to the punctuation. The internal contradiction with detail_evidence (which cites the 39.9% error from Domain 3) is closed: detail_verdict_text now explicitly acknowledges Domain 3 exists and tracks scalar drift rate. Decider commit 539f4228 applied the fix.
patch_verification.c3_hole_2_status: UNCHANGED — open as ISS-1878 (decider acknowledged in queue push)
patch_verification.c3_hole_2_evidence: Current detail_evidence still bundles Livermore-2020 with WMM2025 to attribute 'recent deceleration (from ~55 km/yr peak to ~40 km/yr by 2025)'. The Livermore 2020 paper title is 'Recent north magnetic pole acceleration towards Siberia caused by flux lobe elongation' — its observational scope is the 1990s–2019 acceleration era, not the post-2019 deceleration era. The mechanism (flux-lobe rebalancing) is correctly attributable to Livermore; the post-2019 deceleration observation is post-paper. Re-flagged below.
patch_verification.c3_hole_3_evidence: Cross-ref 'Section 6.5' still present in detail_evidence. Section 6.5 is titled 'The 4 Acknowledged Failures — 3 Refined, 1 Falsified...' (refined-predictions framing). Section 6.6 is 'The Accuracy Claim' (asymmetric scorecard framing). The current text invokes 'tolerance-gaming and asymmetric scorecard' — 6.5 covers tolerance-gaming-via-refinement; 6.6 covers asymmetric scorecard. Either '(Sections 6.5–6.6)' or 'Part 6' is more accurate than '(Section 6.5)' alone.

relevant_files: raw-text/08-live-power.txt:60-65 — Domain 3 NMP Drift Rate, Formula: rate=55*exp(-0.08*(year-2015)) km/yr, Falsification: 'Error >50% for 3 consecutive years'
raw-text/08-live-power.txt:209-210 — Domain: NMP Longitude (Predicted 139.3°E, Observed 138.057°E, Tolerance 5.0%, Status PASS)

raw-text/08-live-power.txt:256-258 — Domain: NMP Drift Rate (Predicted/Observed adaptive, Tolerance 50.0%, Status PASS at 39.9% error). Note: 'Adaptive tolerance — originally tighter, widened when predictions missed'
raw-text/08-live-power.txt:293 — Author's own 'TOLERANCE GAMING' acknowledgment for NMP Drift Rate
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Identical to c3: Domain 3 evaluates rate=55*exp(-0.08*(year-2015)). For 2026 → 22.81 km/yr predicted. Observed pole-speed ~38 km/yr (NOAA WMM2025). |22.81 − 38|/38 = 0.399 (matches dome-reported 39.9% error). The deceleration constant 0.08 and seed value 55 are curve-fit parameters; the dome does not derive them from disc_radius (20,015 km), firmament_height (9,086 km), or aetheric properties. No new code paths introduced by the c3 patch — the patch is purely textual.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Domain 3 is computed from a 2-parameter exponential fit; Domain NMP Longitude is a single-point comparison (predicted scalar 139.3°E vs observed scalar 138.057°E). The dashboard labels Domain 3 'adaptive' (tolerance widened post-misses). No dedicated monitoring entry exists for the WIN-059 sector-specific qualifier; together the two domains monitor (a) deceleration rate and (b) longitude position, but they do not jointly constitute a dome-derived prediction that decel is concentrated in a particular azimuthal sector.
data_sources_used: NOAA NP.xy / WMM2025 (same dataset as WIN-006/007/036/043/063)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: (1) Tolerance widened to 50% from an originally tighter (unspecified) value — author's own dashboard note. (2) Falsification criterion requires error >50% for THREE consecutive years (temporal escape hatch). (3) 'Siberian sector' is geographically undefined, allowing retroactive boundary redrawing. None of these were introduced or removed by the c3 patch.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None remaining. The c3-flagged contradiction (detail_evidence citing Domain 3's 39.9% while detail_verdict said no monitoring entry existed) is resolved. Current detail_verdict_text accurately describes Domain 3 as 'tracks only the scalar drift rate (currently 39.9% off)' and Domain NMP Longitude as 'tracks only a single position scalar' — both factually verified against raw-text/08-live-power.txt:209 and :256–258.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: WIN-059's specific claim is sector-bound deceleration. Domain 3 (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:60-65, 256-258) monitors a scalar deceleration rate but does not decompose by azimuth. Domain NMP Longitude (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:209) monitors one longitude scalar at 5% tolerance but is a position estimate, not a deceleration mechanism. Neither monitors the WIN's sector-bound interpretation as a dome-derived prediction. Tag 'none' is consistent with current evidence text. (Borderline: a defender will argue Domain 3 + Domain NMP Longitude together cover the WIN's compound claim — see hole-1-residual below.)
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: NMP deceleration is documented by NOAA WMM2020/WMM2025 updates and explained mechanistically by Livermore et al. 2020 (flux-lobe rebalancing on the core-mantle boundary; verified DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9). The dome renames the same observational pattern as 'aetheric damping' without altering the numerical curve.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Livermore 2020 (Nature Geoscience) was published May 2020 — predates the dome's V51 WIN list. NOAA out-of-cycle WMM2020 update (Chulliat et al. 2019) had already documented the speed change. The dome's curve-fit formula adopts the post-2020 deceleration trend retroactively.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: rate=55*exp(-0.08*(year-2015)) is a 2-parameter exponential fit to NOAA NP.xy data; the dome's own dashboard labels it 'adaptive.' The dome's H(r)=8537*exp(-r/8619) field is axially symmetric and has no azimuthal degree of freedom from which a Siberian-sector preference could be derived. 'Siberian sector' is borrowed terminology from Livermore 2020's title.

holes_found.description: RESIDUAL FROM HOLE-1 PATCH — the new phrasing 'neither validates that the deceleration is sector-bound to Siberia rather than uniformly distributed' carries an awkwardness a defender will exploit. Domain NMP Longitude monitors observed=138.057°E vs predicted=139.3°E, and 130–140°E IS the Siberian sector by ordinary geographic convention (eastern Russia / east of Lake Baikal). A defender will say: 'Your own text concedes I'm tracking a longitude in the Siberian sector. You can't simultaneously cite my Domain NMP Longitude as a single position scalar AND deny that I'm monitoring sector position.' The reviewer's deeper point is correct (the dome's axially symmetric H(r) cannot DERIVE a sector-bound deceleration mechanism from geometry, so even if both scalars pass, the conjunction is not a dome-derived prediction). But the 'uniformly distributed' framing is conceptually muddled — the NMP is one point, so it's not 'uniformly distributed' in any literal sense; the relevant contrast is 'azimuthally privileged-by-dome-physics vs azimuthally arbitrary-but-monitored.' This is residual moderate severity, not blocking, but worth tightening.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten the contested clause. Suggested rewrite: '...Domain NMP Longitude tracks only a single position scalar (138.057°E observed vs 139.3°E predicted) — but the dome's axially symmetric H(r) field has no azimuthal degree of freedom, so neither domain validates a dome-DERIVED prediction that deceleration would be concentrated in any particular sector; both numbers are adopted from the NOAA pole-position record after the fact.' This preserves the post-hoc point while sidestepping the 'sector-bound vs uniformly distributed' awkwardness. Alternative compact rewrite: 'neither domain treats the Siberian-sector qualifier as a dome-derived prediction; both numbers are adopted from NOAA's published trajectory.' Either rewrite preempts the longitude-tracks-the-sector counterattack.

holes_found.description: LIVERMORE-2020 CITATION PRECISION (carried from c3 Hole-2; ISS-1878 open). Current detail_evidence: 'The NMP's trajectory toward Siberia and its recent deceleration (from ~55 km/yr peak to ~40 km/yr by 2025) are explained by competition between Canadian and Siberian magnetic flux lobes on the core-mantle boundary (Livermore et al., 2020, Nature Geoscience; WMM2025).' The verified Livermore paper title is 'Recent north magnetic pole acceleration towards Siberia caused by flux lobe elongation' (DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9, Nat. Geosci. 13, 387–391, May 2020). Its observational content is the 1990s–2019 acceleration phase; the post-2019 deceleration is post-paper. A defender will say: 'You cite a paper about acceleration to support the deceleration claim. Either the mechanism applies to both phases — in which case say so — or you've miscited.' Decider has acknowledged this in the queue push and via ISS-1878.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) narrow the Livermore citation to mechanism-only and add a deceleration-era source. Suggested: '...explained by competition between Canadian and Siberian magnetic flux lobes on the core-mantle boundary (Livermore et al., 2020, Nat. Geosci., for the flux-lobe mechanism; NOAA WMM2025 release notes documenting the post-2019 deceleration phase). The same flux-lobe rebalancing model accounts for both the 1990s–2010s acceleration and the post-2019 slowdown — relative lobe strengths change in either direction.' (b) OR a one-clause cite: '...Livermore et al., 2020, Nat. Geosci., identifies the flux-lobe mechanism that drives both the 1990s–2010s acceleration and (per subsequent NOAA WMM2025 updates) the post-2019 deceleration phase.' Either form preempts the 'wrong era' attack.

holes_found.description: SECTION 6.5 CROSS-REF (carried from c3 Hole-3, c2 Hole-5). Current detail_evidence: '...this is precisely the tolerance-gaming and asymmetric scorecard pattern identified throughout this review (Section 6.5).' Section 6.5 (verified from data/sections.json:part6) is titled 'The 4 Acknowledged Failures — 3 Refined, 1 Falsified, and a 5th That Doesn\'t Count' — covers tolerance-gaming via refinement. Section 6.6 ('The Accuracy Claim') is the asymmetric-scorecard argument proper. The current text correctly references the tolerance-gaming half (6.5) but misses the asymmetric-scorecard half (6.6).
holes_found.recommendation: Optional minor edit. Replace '(Section 6.5)' with '(Sections 6.5–6.6)' or '(see Part 6)'. Defender unlikely to attack on this; only worth touching if patching this WIN anyway.

stronger_arguments.description: QUANTITATIVE EXTENSION — gap WIDENS over time (carried from c3, still applicable). Dome formula rate=55*exp(-0.08*(year-2015)) projects 22.81 km/yr (2026), 21.06 (2027), 19.44 (2028). Observed pole speed remains ~35–40 km/yr. The dome's curve has the wrong slope, not just a passable offset: by 2028 the dome predicts roughly half the observed rate. Promoting 'gap widens with time' framing into WIN-059 itself would strengthen the kill-shot connection.

NULL-AZIMUTH BIND (refined from c3 'two-domain bind'). The dome simultaneously claims (a) NMP Longitude near 139.3°E (Domain NMP Longitude) and (b) sector-specific deceleration toward Siberia (WIN-059). Both claims rest on azimuth-distinguishing predictions. The dome's H(r)=8,537·exp(−r/8,619) has zero azimuthal terms — for any radius r, every azimuth gives the same field. So either (i) the longitude 139.3°E is unprincipled (adopted from NOAA, not derived) and the longitude PASS is post-hoc, OR (ii) some unstated azimuthal mechanism exists that the dome never publishes. Both options are fatal: (i) makes the longitude claim non-predictive; (ii) requires hidden machinery that violates 'zero free parameters.' This bind sharpens the symmetry critique by anchoring it to a SECOND monitored claim.
sources: raw-text/05-model.txt — H(r) functional form (no azimuthal terms)

kernel_of_truth.description: The NMP HAS decelerated since ~2019 from a ~55 km/yr peak to ~35–40 km/yr today (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:256-258 reports the dome's tracked 39.9% error against ~38 km/yr observed — confirming the decel reality even as the dome's specific formula misses by 39.9%). The trajectory IS toward Siberia (Livermore 2020 verified-DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9, paper title contains 'towards Siberia caused by flux lobe elongation'). The exponential functional form rate=55*exp(-0.08*(year-2015)) qualitatively captures deceleration and gives the right sign. The dome correctly identifies that pole behavior changed character around the 2010s.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Four problems remain (mostly unchanged from c3). (1) Post-hoc adoption — Livermore 2020 (DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9) and the NOAA WMM2020 out-of-cycle update predate the dome's WIN-059 — code_analysis_tags.post_hoc=true. (2) The deceleration formula's parameters (55, 0.08) are curve-fit, not derived from disc_radius, firmament_height, or aetheric quantities (data/wins.json:WIN-059.code_analysis.derives_from_dome=false, confirmed in c4). (3) The dome's axially symmetric H(r) (raw-text/05-model.txt) has no azimuthal mechanism — any 'sector' label is borrowed (relabels_standard=true). (4) The dome's own monitoring (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:256-258) shows 39.9% error and only PASSes because tolerance was widened to 50% from an admittedly tighter prior value — making the prediction unfalsifiable by methodological design (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:293 'TOLERANCE GAMING' is the author's own term).

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender accepting our six methodological principles after the c3 patch lands would push two points (down from c3's three, since the absolute-overreach point is closed). (1) The new detail_verdict_text says 'Domain NMP Longitude tracks only a single position scalar (138.057°E observed vs 139.3°E predicted) — neither validates that the deceleration is sector-bound to Siberia rather than uniformly distributed.' But 138.057°E IS in the Siberian sector by simple geography (Sakha Republic, eastern Russia). Your own text inadvertently concedes that I'm monitoring a longitude in the Siberian sector. The 'uniformly distributed' framing is conceptually muddled — the pole is one point, not a distribution. (2) On Livermore: you cite a paper titled 'Recent north magnetic pole ACCELERATION towards Siberia' to support the dome's deceleration claim. Either the flux-lobe model accommodates both phases (then say so explicitly) or you've miscited the era. Together these two leave a defender with material to argue: 'reviewer concedes my longitude monitoring AND miscites the literature.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Two small text changes drop the defense rating to 0–1. (a) Tighten the sector-bound clause as recommended in Hole-1-residual: replace 'sector-bound to Siberia rather than uniformly distributed' with 'a dome-DERIVED prediction that deceleration would be concentrated in any particular sector; both numbers are adopted from NOAA's pole-position record.' This converts the longitude-monitoring concession into the structural critique it should be (axial symmetry has no azimuthal terms). (b) Add the deceleration-era source on Livermore as recommended in Hole-2: cite Livermore for the mechanism, NOAA WMM2025 for the post-2019 observation. With both edits, the only residual defense is the (i) cluster-data-reuse point and (ii) the kill-shot tolerance-gaming point — both of which are already structurally closed.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: No factual contradictions detected across the NMP/SAA cluster. Tag heterogeneity (WIN-006/007 monitoring=live_fetch, WIN-036=hardcoded, WIN-043/059/063=none) remains internally defensible by the rule 'live_fetch when monitor.py actually fetches the WIN-claimed value; none when the WIN's specific interpretive claim has no dedicated entry.' The c3-c4 patch did not change WIN-059's monitoring tag (still 'none') and did not introduce inconsistency with sibling WINs. NOTE for future polish: WIN-059 detail_evidence references the cluster ('part of group WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063') but does not explicitly cross-reference the methodology shared with WIN-006/007. Adding 'see WIN-007/043 for the same NOAA-NP.xy data reuse pattern' would tighten cluster coherence — not blocking, stronger-arguments territory.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 4 — Verify-Patch Re-Review (queue_id=311, decider commit 539f4228 2026-05-08T05:21Z)

**Triggering event.** Decider self-applied c3 Hole-1 fix and pushed WIN-059 to the priority queue for adversarial verification. Commit message explicitly enumerates: Hole-1 fixed (monitoring overstatement), Hole-2 remaining (Livermore citation precision, ISS-1878), Hole-3 remaining (Section 6.5 cross-ref, minor).

**Patch verification.** Direct substring check confirms the old contradictory absolute ('no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all') has been REMOVED, and the c3-recommended replacement ('no monitoring entry validates the WIN-specific claim — Domain 3 tracks only the scalar drift rate (currently 39.9% off), and Domain NMP Longitude tracks only a single position scalar (138.057°E observed vs 139.3°E predicted); neither validates that the deceleration is sector-bound to Siberia rather than uniformly distributed.') is PRESENT. The internal contradiction with detail_evidence (which cites the 39.9% error from precisely the Domain 3 entry) is closed: the new text explicitly acknowledges Domain 3 exists. Quality of the patch: very high — the decider applied the c3 reviewer's recommended language nearly verbatim, including the parenthetical numerical citations.

**New residual issue (Hole-1-residual, moderate).** The new phrasing inherits an awkwardness from the c3 reviewer's own draft: 'neither validates that the deceleration is sector-bound to Siberia rather than uniformly distributed.' The 'uniformly distributed' framing is conceptually muddled — the NMP is a single point, not a distribution. More importantly, the Domain NMP Longitude IS effectively monitoring sector position: 138.057°E lies squarely in the Siberian sector by ordinary geographic convention (eastern Russia / Sakha Republic). A defender in advocate-mode will exploit this: 'You concede I'm tracking a longitude IN the Siberian sector. You can't simultaneously cite Domain NMP Longitude as just a position scalar AND deny that it monitors the sector dimension.' The reviewer's deeper point — that the dome's axially symmetric H(r) cannot DERIVE a sector-bound deceleration mechanism, so even passing both domains doesn't constitute a dome-derived prediction — is correct, but the current wording obscures it. Recommended rewrite (in Hole-1-residual.recommendation) preserves the structural critique while sidestepping the longitude-tracks-the-sector counterattack. Severity: moderate (defender has a real wedge but the structural axial-symmetry rebuttal is intact). This residual emerged from c3's recommendation as-applied; not the decider's fault.

**Hole-2 unchanged.** Decider explicitly carried Hole-2 forward as ISS-1878 open. Current detail_evidence still bundles Livermore-2020 with WMM2025 to attribute the 'recent deceleration.' Verified Livermore paper title (via DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9): 'Recent north magnetic pole ACCELERATION towards Siberia caused by flux lobe elongation' — observational scope is the 1990s–2019 acceleration phase. Re-flagged at moderate. Recommendation: cite Livermore for mechanism, NOAA WMM2025 release notes for post-2019 observation, OR add a one-clause 'mechanism accommodates both phases' qualifier.

**Hole-3 unchanged.** Section 6.5 ('The 4 Acknowledged Failures...') covers tolerance-gaming-via-refinement; Section 6.6 ('The Accuracy Claim') is the asymmetric-scorecard argument proper. Current text invokes 'tolerance-gaming and asymmetric scorecard pattern' — naturally references both. Either '(Sections 6.5–6.6)' or '(see Part 6)' is more accurate than '(Section 6.5)' alone. Re-flagged at minor; optional polish.

**Verdict assessment.** 'Std Model Explains' continues to hold. The c3-c4 patch did not change verdict-relevant facts. The dome's NMP drift prediction is still failing at 39.9%; the dome's geometry is still axially symmetric; the recent deceleration is still explained by standard flux-lobe dynamics; the data is still NOAA NP.xy reused across the NMP cluster. No verdict change recommended.

**Tag stability.** All four code_analysis tags unchanged from c3 (monitoring=none, relabels_standard=true, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false). The c3-c4 patch was textual; no tag-relevant code paths changed. Re-validated against current evidence text: all four tags consistent.

**Cross-WIN consistency.** No new inconsistencies introduced. WIN-059 remains in the WIN-006/007/036/043/059/063 NMP cluster sharing NOAA NP.xy data; tag heterogeneity (live_fetch vs hardcoded vs none) is internally defensible.

**Counter-perspective (per user instruction to consider the other side).** A fair-minded dome defender accepting our methodology would push: (i) the longitude-monitors-the-sector point above, (ii) the Livermore-era-mismatch point above, (iii) that the dome's qualitative pattern (NMP decelerating, trajectory toward Siberia) is real and matches observation, even if specific dome parameters miss the rate. Point (iii) is acknowledged in our kernel_of_truth and doesn't undermine the verdict. Points (i) and (ii) are the residual moderate holes flagged in this review.

**Priority for decider.** Hole-1-residual (moderate) is the highest-impact residual — the patch closed the c3 contradiction but inherited an awkward phrase that lets a defender wedge in. A small targeted text edit (5-10 words) closes it. Hole-2 (carried as ISS-1878) is the next priority — wider scope (citation refinement plus a new source). Hole-3 is optional polish. None require verdict change. Patch-verification gate: PASS for c3 Hole-1; the patch did what it was designed to do, with the noted residual.
